title: AIMS prompt-regression history
description: Schema reference for validate_prompt_regression.py. Validation is implemented in Python rather than a JSON Schema library to avoid runtime dependencies. Written by aims.prompt_regression to data/performance/prompt_regressions.json (#97): prompt/model version history recorded alongside measured structural results.

notes.purpose: Every prompt or model change must run through aims.prompt_regression before adoption; a CI test asserts that the committed prompt file's SHA-256 plus the pinned model ID have a recorded passing entry, so a prompt edit without a recorded harness run fails CI.
notes.keying: model_id, prompt_version, and prompt_sha256 describe the configuration under test (the committed constants in aims.qualitative and the committed prompt file); artifact_provenance echoes what the evaluated artifact itself was generated with — they differ when the harness runs over committed fixtures instead of freshly generated candidates.
notes.metrics: Structural and gate metrics only (validator error count, gate pass rates, stance/confidence distributions, citation coverage, structure counts) — never exact prose, so wording changes alone cannot fail the harness.
notes.checks: Named boolean assertions with thresholds (validation_clean, market_narrative_rendered, instrument_gate_pass_rate, citation_coverage, stance_distribution_sane); 'passed' must equal the conjunction of all checks.
notes.determinism: Entries are inserted or replaced by their (prompt_version, prompt_sha256, model_id, analysis_date, artifact) key and kept sorted, so re-running the harness on the same inputs is idempotent.
notes.non_goals: No automatic prompt tuning or optimization loops; the harness measures and records, adoption stays a reviewed human decision.
